text.title: vui-iot-integration Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the vui-iot-integration methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Devices MUST be grouped into rooms, zones, and scenes with stable IDs. Per-device-only naming is forbidden for households with ≥5 devices.
rule.rationale: Per-device naming forces users to memorize unique names; grouping leverages spatial reasoning.

rule.statement: Scenes and routines MUST have intuitive natural-language names (e.g., 'movie night', 'good morning'). Acronyms and IDs are forbidden as primary names.

rationale.source: Apple HomeKit guidelines; Google Home guidelines

rule.rationale: Voice users invoke by natural names; IDs and acronyms fail discoverability.

rule.statement: Multi-device commands MUST report per-device outcomes explicitly when any device fails ('Lights on; thermostat unreachable'). Silent partial failures are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent failures hide broken state; users assume command succeeded and act on false belief.

rule.statement: Every voice routine MUST support 'undo' or 'reverse last action' for at least the previous command. Permanent state changes without undo are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Voice mistakes (wrong room, wrong device) need a single-command recovery path.

rule.statement: Routines MUST be tested in noisy conditions (kitchen, TV, multiple speakers). Pure-quiet testing is forbidden as evidence.
rule.rationale: Smart-home contexts are noisy; quiet-only testing inflates real accuracy.
